definition:
κανών, -όνος, ὁ
 (cf. κάννα, and Heb. קָנֶה, a reed), [in LXX: Mic.7:4, Jdth.13:6, 4Ma.7:21 (and in Aq., Psa.19:5, Job.38:5)* ;] 
  1. a rod or bar (Jth, l.with). 
   2. a measuring rule; hence, metaph, 
   3. a rule or standard: Gal.6:16. 
   4. a limit (RV, province): 2Co.10:13-16. (For the history of the word and esp. its later meanings, see Westc., Canon, App. A; cf. also MM, Exp., xv; Cremer, 744.)†
 (AS)
